name: Transfer Learning
oneliner: Reusing a model pretrained on a large general corpus as the starting point for a new, often smaller, downstream task.
explanation: Transfer learning is the practice of taking a model trained on a broad dataset and adapting it to a specific task, rather than training from scratch. The pretrained weights already encode grammar, facts, and reasoning patterns, so the downstream task needs far less data and compute. GPT-3 demonstrated that very large models can transfer to new tasks with zero or few examples via in-context learning, without any weight updates. Transfer learning is the conceptual foundation underlying all modern fine-tuning methods — full fine-tuning, LoRA, QLoRA, and prefix tuning are all strategies for how much of the pretrained weights to adapt.
fundamentals: Transfer assumes features learned on $P_{\text{source}}(x)$ are useful for $P_{\text{target}}(x)$. Fine-tuning updates $\theta_{\text{pretrained}} \to \theta + \Delta\theta$ using a task-specific loss. PEFT methods constrain $\Delta\theta$: LoRA uses $\Delta W = BA$ with $B \in \mathbb{R}^{d \times r}$, $r \ll d$, reducing trainable params from $O(d^2)$ to $O(rd)$.

foundational_papers.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers

resources.label: Sebastian Ruder — Transfer Learning in NLP
